Items.Title: 揭秘 Postman Web 的工作原理 | Demystifying How Postman Web works
Items.Description:
它从一个后来被弃用的 Chrome 扩展程序开始，因此他们转移到了桌面应用程序中。 然而，在 HoppScotch 开源基于 Web 的 Rest API 客户端成功之后，Postman 也推出了他们基于 Web 的 API 客户端。

但是你有没有想过 Postman 在幕后是如何运作的？ 幸运的是，如今调试 Web 应用程序轻而易举，因为全面的 Chrome DevTools 使您能够解构 Web 上的任何应用程序。 在本文中，我们将了解 Postman Web 的工作原理和 HoppScotch 的工作原理非常相似。

我们将深入研究 Postman 的 Web 应用程序的内部工作原理，并探讨当您使用它来测试 HTTP 请求时会发生什么。 为此，我们将使用 Requestly Mock Server 创建几个虚拟 API 端点。 然后，我们将使用浏览器 DevTools 的网络选项卡分析 Postman 的行为，从而允许我们解构应用程序的操作。

设置模拟服务器

如果您以前使用过 Postman，这应该看起来很熟悉。 今天我们将处理两个简单的请求——一个 GET 请求和一个 POST 请求。 然后，我们将检查 chrome 开发工具中的网络选项卡，以确定 Postman 如何与我们测试的实际 API 端点进行通信。

首先，让我们使用 Requestly 快速设置一个模拟服务器——一个开源浏览器扩展，可以快速跟踪您的 Web 调试过程。 我们可以用它来模拟我们的测试 API。 安装后，转到 Requestly 的模拟服务器并单击创建新的模拟 API。

这应该会打开一个简单的表单，您可以在其中指定 API 和模拟服务器的详细信息。 让我们选择 Method GET，Endpoint 作为 Postman_GET（您可以随意放置它），并在正文中有一个简单的示例响应。 之后，单击右上角的“创建”按钮。 然后，Requestly 应该在您现在可以与 Postman 一起使用的 URL 部分下为您生成一个模拟 API 端点：

伟大的！ 我们设法在几分钟内使用 Requestly 启动了一个模拟 API 服务器。 但这并不是您可以使用 Requestly 做的全部。 有趣的是，您还可以使用 Requestly 检查网络流量以分析 HTTP 请求。

免费试用 Requestly！

检查网络流量

为了检查来自 Postman 桌面应用程序的流量，我们可以使用连接源中可用的系统范围代理选项。

但是，要检查来自 Postman 网络的流量，我们可以启动一个新的 Chrome 实例并获取所有流量。 以下是如何从 Requestly Desktop 应用程序启动新的 Chrome 实例：

然后您实际上可以检查该 chrome 实例上的所有网络流量：

这确实很酷！ 您可以根据特定域进一步过滤流量，并使用 Requestly 修改来自 Postman web 的网络流量。 然而，由于这里的要求只是检查网络流量而不是修改它，我们将使用 Chrome 开发工具，所以让我们继续吧。

使用 Postman Web 发送请求

让我们使用刚刚在 Postman Web 中创建的 Mock API 端点，并在将请求类型设置为“GET”后点击“发送”按钮：

但这是怎么发生的呢？ 解码使用 Postman Web 发送的 GET 请求

为了解码这个 GET 请求，打开 chrome 开发工具并转到网络选项卡：

请注意，Postman 发送了两个请求。 第二个名为“request”的请求是我们真正感兴趣的。让我们单击它并进一步检查该请求：

请注意 Postman 如何向 URL https://orion-http.gw.postman.co/v1/request发送 POST 请求，并且请求标头中指定的来源是https://web.postman.co 。 奇怪的是，Postman 没有直接将请求发送给原始调用者或 API URL https://requestly.dev/api/mockv2/Postman_GET?rq_uid=0NkhbAyXpLTtgUo3qMBp26Y6UG12 。 相反，它将请求发送到其他地方，一个托管在 Postman 子域上的不同服务器。

我们稍后会回到上述问题的答案。 让我们预览对此请求的响应并验证我们实际上正在检查正确的请求！

那么，对该请求的响应是从 GET 请求收到的实际响应，因此它是 Postman 发送的正确请求，用于填充 Postman Web 界面上的响应选项卡。 但是等等，邮递员在哪里指定实际 HTTP 请求的 URL？ 尽管这是一个 POST 请求，但我们在请求正文中看不到任何 POST 参数。

让我们进一步向下滚动请求标头。 如果你仔细观察，有一堆以“pm”为前缀的选项：

以下是 Postman 在请求中发送的以“pm”为前缀的请求标头：

请求的方法或类型，在本例中为 GET，在“pm-o0”选项中设置。

看起来 Postman 指定了它通过这些以“pm-”为前缀的自定义标头发送的请求元数据。 有趣的！

解码使用 Postman Web 发送的 POST 请求

我们已经了解了 GET 请求如何与 Postman 一起工作，让我们也了解 POST 请求如何工作。 现在让我们使用 Requestly Mock Server 生成一个 POST 端点：

这与我们生成 GET 端点的方式类似，只是现在 Method 字段将改为 POST。 我们将使用以下 JSON 向新端点发送 POST 请求：

{ "name": "fuzzysid", "job": "developer", “message”: “success” }

让我们发送这个请求，打开网络选项卡，看看请求是什么样子的：

同样，Postman 向其自己的服务器发送请求，您现在可以在指定 API URL 的请求标头中看到相同的“pm-”前缀选项。 请求标头中的“pm-u”现在对应于新端点。 此外，此请求的有效负载是发送给原始请求的实际有效负载：

一旦您设法解码 GET 请求，POST 请求看起来就更容易理解了。

为什么Postman会向自己的Server发送请求？

当您使用 Postman 发送 GET 和 POST 请求时，检查网络选项卡并尝试弄清楚幕后发生的事情很有趣。 但现在终于到了向房间里的大象讲话的时候了。

为什么 Postman 不能直接向我们要测试的端点直接发送 HTTP 请求呢？

答案在于所有请求都通过浏览器通过 Postman 的 Web 应用程序。 因此，通过 Postman Web 发出的任何请求都必须首先通过浏览器。

进行此安全检查是为了防止恶意脚本在用户不知情的情况下代表用户执行操作。 因此，当通过 Postman 发出请求时，它会被浏览器拦截，浏览器会检查发出请求的域是否与发出请求的域相同。 如果域不匹配，浏览器会阻止请求并抛出 CORS 错误。

从视觉上看，CORS 可以帮助您更好地理解：

在我们的例子中，请求中的 referrer 表示发送请求的域，格式为https://web.postman.co/workspace/My-Workspace~96d4bc9b-cb4e-4202-8436-5c966d5290fe/request/create?requestId=7922be58-fd6b-45e8-8e12-19609d5c6be2&ctx=code

这意味着请求从上述 URL 发送到我们的 Requestly Mock 端点。 请求的来源或引用者和请求 URL 都在不同的域中。 因此，如果 Postman 尝试直接发送此请求，浏览器将抛出 CORS 错误。

为了绕过此限制，Postman 将此请求发送到其代理服务器https://orion-http.gw.postman.co/v1/request ，然后代理服务器将请求转发到预期的端点。 此代理服务器与 Postman 发送的请求中的来源或引荐来源网址具有相同的子域。 在收到来自 Postman Web 的请求后，代理服务器通过请求标头中的“pm-”选项从请求中提取端点、有效负载等。 来自端点的响应然后被中继回 Postman Web 应用程序，然后将其转发到客户端的浏览器。 这样，请求似乎来自同一个域，并且 CORS 安全检查得到满足，允许请求继续没有任何问题。

结论

今天，我们不仅全面了解了 Postman Web 的功能，还轻松探索了对 Web 应用程序进行逆向工程的艺术。 通过 DevTools 和 Requestly Mock Server 深入研究 Postman web 的复杂性是一种引人入胜的体验，而且从这里还有很多东西有待发现。 然而，仍有一些悬而未决的问题可能需要进一步探索。

例如，当 Postman 已经将代理请求作为 POST 请求发送时，为什么不使用 POST 参数来定义 API URL 和其他元数据？ 此外，自定义标头中的“pm-”前缀可能代表“Postman”，但自定义标头选项中的后缀（例如“pm-o0”中的“o0”）可能仍然不清楚。 在 HTTP 请求中定义自定义标头时，是否必须遵循特定的命名约定？

当您思考这些问题时，请务必继续关注我们即将发布的有关新鲜和令人兴奋的内容的指南。



原文:

Postman is an exceptionally well-regarded REST API client that developers rely on to construct and evaluate their APIs. It started with a Chrome extension which later got deprecated, so they moved into the desktop app. However, after the success of the HoppScotch Open Source Web-based Rest API client, Postman also launched their web-based API client.

But have you ever contemplated how Postman operates behind the scenes? Fortunately, debugging web applications today is a breeze, as the comprehensive Chrome DevTools enable you to deconstruct any application on the web. In this article, we will learn how Postman Web works and HoppScotch indeed works very similarly.

We will delve into the inner workings of Postman's web application and explore what transpires when you use it to test your HTTP requests. To do this, we'll use Requestly Mock Server to create a couple of dummy API endpoints. We'll then analyze the behaviour of Postman using the network tab of our browser's DevTools, allowing us to deconstruct the application's operations.

Setting up Mock Server

If you’ve ever used Postman before, this should appear familiar. We’ll work with two simple requests today - a GET request and a POST request. We’ll then inspect the network tab in the chrome dev tools to figure out how Postman communicates with the actual API endpoint that we test.

First, let’s quickly set up a mock server with Requestly - an open source browser extension that fast tracks your web debugging process. We can it use to simulate our test APIs. Once installed, head over to Requestly's Mock Server and click on Create new mock API.

That should open a simple form where you can specify details of the API and the mock server. Let’s choose the Method GET, Endpoint as Postman_GET (you can put this literally whatever you want) and have a simple sample response in the body. After that, click on the “Create” button at the top right corner. Then, Requestly should generate a mock API endpoint for you under the URL section that you can now use with Postman:

Great! We managed to spin up a mock API server with Requestly in minutes. But that’s not all you can do with Requestly. Interestingly, you can also inspect network traffic with Requestly to analyse HTTP requests.

Try out Requestly for free!

Inspecting Network Traffic

Requestly Desktop App offers Inspecting & Modifying network traffic from browsers, mobile apps, desktop apps, Node server & Terminal. In order to inspect traffic from Postman desktop app, we can use System-wide proxy option available in Connected source.

However, to inspect traffic from Postman web, we can launch a new Chrome instance and get all the traffic. Here’s how you can launch a new Chrome instance from Requestly Desktop app:

And then you can actually inspect all the network traffic on that chrome instance:

That is pretty cool indeed! You can further filter the traffic based on a specific domain and modify network traffic from Postman web using Requestly. However, since the requirement here is only to inspect network traffic and not modify it, we will use Chrome dev tools so let’s move ahead with that.

Sending a request with Postman Web

Let’s use the Mock API endpoint we just created in Postman Web and hit the “Send” button after setting the request type to “GET”:

Postman displays the response from the API and it’s the same response that we defined. But how does this happen? What does Postman do behind the scenes?

Decoding the GET Request sent with Postman Web

In order to decode this GET request, open chrome dev tools and go to the network tab:

Notice that there are two requests being sent by Postman. The second request with the name “request” is what we’re actually interested in. Let’s click on it and inspect that request further:

Notice how Postman sends a POST request to the URL https://orion-http.gw.postman.co/v1/request and the origin specified in the request headers is https://web.postman.co. It’s strange that Postman is not directly sending the request to the original caller or the API URL https://requestly.dev/api/mockv2/Postman_GET?rq_uid=0NkhbAyXpLTtgUo3qMBp26Y6UG12. Instead, it’s sending the request somewhere else, a different server hosted on Postman’s sub-domain.

We’ll come back to the answer to the above question in a bit. Let’s preview the response to this request and verify that we’re actually inspecting the correct request!

Well, the response to this request is the actual response received from the GET request so it is the correct request that Postman sent to populate the response tab on the Postman web interface. But wait, where did postman specify the URL of the actual HTTP request? Even though this is a POST request, we don’t see any POST parameters in the body of the request.

Let’s scroll down further on the request headers. If you look closely, there are a bunch of options that are prefixed with “pm”:

These are the following request headers prefixed with “pm” that Postman sent in the request:

The “pm-u” option is actually the part where Postman has specified the API URL to which the actual request is sent. The method or the type of request, which in this case is GET is set in the “pm-o0” option.

It looks like Postman specifies the request metadata it sends via these custom headers prefixed by “pm-”. Interesting!

Decoding a POST Request sent with Postman Web

We’ve seen how a GET request works with Postman, let’s also see how a POST request works. Let’s now use the Requestly Mock Server to generate a POST endpoint now:

This is a similar way to how we generated the GET endpoint only now the Method field would be POST instead. We’ll send a POST request to the new endpoint with the following JSON:

{ "name": "fuzzysid", "job": "developer", “message”: “success” }

Let’s send this request, open the network tab and see what the request looks like:

Again, Postman sends a request to its own server and you can now see the same “pm-” prefixed options in the request header specifying the API URL. The “pm-u” in the request header now corresponds to the new endpoint. The “pm-o0” option now specifies the method as POST.

Further, the payload to this request is the actual payload sent to the original request:

Looks like a POST request is more or less straightforward to understand once you’ve managed to decode a GET request.

Why does Postman send requests to their own Server?

It was fun inspecting the network tab and trying to make sense of what’s happening under the hood when you send a GET and POST request with Postman. But it’s finally time to address the elephant in the room.

Why can’t Postman directly send an HTTP request to the endpoint we want to test directly?

The answer lies in the fact that all requests go through Postman's web application via the browser. Consequently, any request made through Postman web must pass through the browser first.

CORS

The reason why Postman can't send an HTTP request directly to the endpoint that you want to test is due to the Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) mechanism implemented by web browsers. CORS is a security feature implemented in web browsers that prevent web pages from making requests to a different domain than the one that the page originated from. This security check is in place to prevent malicious scripts from performing actions on behalf of the user without their knowledge. So when a request is made through Postman, it is intercepted by the browser, which checks whether the domain that the request is being made to is the same as the one from which the request originated. If the domains do not match, the browser blocks the request and throws a CORS error.

Visually here’s what CORS is to help you understand better:

In our case, the referrer in the requests represents the domain from which the request is sent which is of the form https://web.postman.co/workspace/My-Workspace~96d4bc9b-cb4e-4202-8436-5c966d5290fe/request/create?requestId=7922be58-fd6b-45e8-8e12-19609d5c6be2&ctx=code

That means requests are sent from the above URL to our Requestly Mock endpoints. The origin or the referrer of the request and the request URL both are on different domains. Therefore the browser will throw a CORS error if Postman tries to directly send this request.

To bypass this restriction, Postman sends this request to its proxy server https://orion-http.gw.postman.co/v1/request which then forwards the request to the intended endpoint. This proxy server has the same sub-domain as the origin or referrer in the request Postman sends. On receiving the request from Postman web, the proxy server extracts endpoints, payload etc from the request via the “pm-” options in the request header. The response from the endpoint is then relayed back to the Postman web application which then forwards it to the client's browser. This way, the request appears to come from the same domain, and the CORS security check is satisfied, allowing the request to proceed without any issues.

Conclusion

Today, we not only gained a comprehensive understanding of how Postman web functions, but also explored the art of reverse engineering a web application with ease. It was an engaging experience delving into the intricacies of Postman web through the DevTools & Requestly Mock Server, and there's still much more to uncover from here. However, there remain a few unanswered questions that may require further exploration.

For example, why does Postman not utilize POST parameters to define the API URL and other metadata when it is already sending the proxy request as a POST request? Additionally, the "pm-" prefix in the custom headers likely stands for "Postman," but the suffixes in the custom header options such as "o0" in "pm-o0" may still be unclear. Is there a particular naming convention that must be followed when defining custom headers in an HTTP request?

As you ponder these questions, be sure to stay tuned for our upcoming guide on something fresh and exciting.

Items.Description:
内容

  • 此部分包括主要内容，例如文本、图像和其他媒体。 高度和宽度特性可以改变。

填充

  • 填充区域是内容区域和边框框之间的空间。 它可以应用于所有面或特定面，例如顶部、右侧、底部和左侧。

边界

  • 边框区域围绕填充和内容，可以应用于所有边或特定边，例如顶部、右侧、底部和左侧。

利润

  • 边距区域是框的边框和外边缘之间的空间。
 <style> div { width: 300px; height: 200px; border: 2 pixels of solid black; padding: 10px; margin: 20px; } </style> <html> <body> <div> some content </div> </body> </html>

在前面的示例中，包含真实内容的 div 元素的最内层的宽度为 300px，padding 属性环绕内容层并在内容和边框之间提供 10px 的空间。

内联 VS 块

块- HTML 块级元素在新行开始并占据其父元素的整个宽度。 它们能够在填充容器的整个宽度时利用宽度、高度和文本对齐属性。

内联- HTML 内联元素始终在同一行开始，它们的宽度由它们的内容决定。 大多数内联元素都是展示性的。

堵塞排队
块级元素可以同时具有内联元素和块元素作为子元素或后代元素内联级别元素只能将内联元素作为子元素或后代元素。
定位绝对/相对

在没有任何这样的祖先的情况下，一个绝对定位的元素被放置在与文档主体相关的位置，并将与页面一起滚动。

需要注意的是，具有绝对定位的元素脱离了页面的正常流动，可能会与其他元素重叠。

如果指定了 top、right、bottom 或 left 属性，元素的位置将相应调整，但周围的内容不会受到影响以填充元素位移所创建的空间。

 div.relative { position: relative; width: 400px; height: 200px; border: 3px solid #73AD21; } div.absolute { position: absolute; top: 80px; right: 0; width: 200px; height: 100px; border: 3px solid #73AD21; }

它们允许您根据特定元素在层次结构中的位置定位特定元素，例如第一个子元素、最后一个子元素或备用元素。 以下是一些常见示例：

  • 当有多个规则针对同一元素时，它是一种用于确定哪个 CSS 规则适用于 HTML 元素的方法。 特异性是根据规则中使用的选择器的类型以及每个选择器在规则中的实例数来计算的。

    类型选择器，

  • 类选择器，
  • 属性选择器。

选择器按特异性递增的顺序排列，类型选择器的特异性最低，ID 选择器的特异性最高。
它在 CSS 中用于将样式应用于不同的设备或屏幕尺寸。 媒体查询允许 Web 开发人员为不同的设备、分辨率和方向指定不同的 CSS 样式。

媒体查询包括一种媒体类型，例如屏幕、打印或语音，以及一个或多个定义应应用样式的条件的表达式。 例如，您可以使用媒体查询将样式仅应用到最大宽度为 768 像素的设备，或者打印为在纸上打印而优化的样式。

 @media (max-width: 768px) { body { background-color: blue; } }

弹性和网格

创建网格后，您可以通过指定应使用哪些网格线和区域来在其中放置内容。 这使得 CSS Grid 对于创建需要高度控制和复杂的布局非常有用，就像您可能在杂志或仪表板上看到的那样。

另一方面，Flexbox 是一个布局模块，它通过创建可用于组织网页上的项目的容器来工作。 我们可以使用 Flexbox 来告诉这些容器应该如何组织其中的项目，比如指定它们应该排成一行还是排成一列。 这使得 Flexbox 对于创建需要更灵活和响应不同屏幕尺寸的布局非常有用，例如导航菜单或图像库。

元标签

元标记提供有关网页的信息，例如其描述、关键字、作者和字符编码。

原文:

The CSS box type is like a container that has the following properties:

Content

  • This section includes the primary content, such as text, images, and other media. The height and width characteristics can be changed.

Padding

  • The padding area is the space between the content region and the border box. It can be applied to all sides or particular sides such as the top, right, bottom, and left.

Border

  • The border area surrounds the padding and content and can be applied to all sides or particular sides such as the top, right, bottom, and left.

Margin

  • The margin region is the space between the border and the outside edge of the box. 

<style>

div {

  width: 300px;

  height: 200px;

border: 2 pixels of solid black;

  padding: 10px;

  margin: 20px;

}

</style>

<html>

<body>

<div>

some content

</div>

</body>

</html>

In the preceding example, the innermost layer of the div element that includes the real content has a width of 300px, and the padding property surrounds the content layer and provides 10px space between the content and the border.

The border property surrounds the padding layer and provides a visible border of 2 px, and the margin property is the outermost layer that surrounds the complete element.

Inline VS Block

Block - HTML Block Level elements commence on a new line and occupy the entire width of their parent element. They have the ability to utilize width, height, and text-align properties as they fill the entire width of their container.

Inline - HTML inline elements consistently begin on the same line and their width is determined by their content. Most inline elements are presentational.

Block Inline
A block level element can have both inline and block elements as children or descendants inline level elements can have only inline elements as children or descendant.
CSS property: display:block CSS property: display:inline

Postioning Absolute/Relative

Aboslute - When an element is assigned a position value of "absolute", its position is determined in relation to the closest ancestor element that has a specified position (as opposed to being positioned relative to the viewport, which is the case for "fixed" elements).

In the absence of any such ancestor, an absolutely positioned element is placed in relation to the document body and will scroll along with the page.

It's important to note that elements with absolute positioning are taken out of the normal flow of the page and may overlap with other elements.

Relative - When an element is assigned a position value of "relative", it will be positioned based on its default position. If the top, right, bottom, or left properties are specified, the element's position will be adjusted accordingly, but the surrounding content will not be affected to fill the space created by the element's displacement.

div.relative {
  position: relative;
  width: 400px;
  height: 200px;
  border: 3px solid #73AD21;
}

div.absolute {
  position: absolute;
  top: 80px;
  right: 0;
  width: 200px;
  height: 100px;
  border: 3px solid #73AD21;
}

CSS Structural Classes

They allow you to target specific elements like the first child, last child, or alternate elements based on their position in the hierarchy. Here are some common examples:

  • :first-child - targets the element that appears first among its siblings.
  • :nth-child(n) - targets elements at a certain position, based on an expression.
  • :last-child- targets the element that appears last among its siblings.
  • :nth-last-child(n) - like :nth-child, but starts counting from the end.
  • :only-child - targets elements that are the only child of their parent element.
  • :first-of-type - targets the first element of a specific type of sibling.
  • :nth-of-type(n) - targets elements of a specific type at a position determined by an expression.
  • :last-of-type - targets the last element of a specific type of sibling. :nth-last-of-type(n) - like :nth-of-type, but starts counting from the end.

CSS Specifity

It is a method used to determine which CSS rule applies to an HTML element when there are multiple rules targeting the same element. Specificity is calculated based on the type of selector used in the rule, and the number of instances of each selector in the rule.

Four types of selectors in CSS:

  • type selectors,
  • class selectors,
  • ID selectors,
  • attribute selectors.

The selectors are ranked in order of increasing specificity, with type selectors having the lowest specificity and ID selectors having the highest.
!important keyword can be applied to override specificity and force a rule to apply to an element, it is better to avoid this keyword.

CSS Responsive Queries

It is used in CSS to apply styles to different devices or screen sizes. Media queries allow web developers to specify different CSS styles for different devices, resolutions, and orientations.

The media query includes a media type, such as screen, print, or speech, and one or more expressions that define the conditions under which the styles should be applied. For example, you can use a media query to apply styles only to devices with a maximum width of 768 pixels, or to print styles that are optimized for printing on paper.

@media (max-width: 768px) {
  body {
    background-color: blue;
  }
}

Flex and Grid

CSS Grid and Flexbox are two different properties that web developers can use to create complex and responsive web layouts.

CSS Grid is a layout module that works by creating a grid of rows and columns on a webpage. Once you've created your grid, you can place content within it by specifying which grid lines and areas should be used. This makes CSS Grid really useful for creating layouts that need to be highly controlled and complex, like those you might see in a magazine or on a dashboard.

Flexbox, on the other hand, is a layout module that works by creating containers that can be used to organize items on a webpage. we can use Flexbox to tell these containers how the items within them should be organized, like by specifying whether they should be laid out in a row or a column. This makes Flexbox really useful for creating layouts that need to be more flexible and responsive to different screen sizes, like a navigation menu or a gallery of images.

Meta Tags

The meta tag provides information about a web page, such as its description, keywords, author, and character encoding.
This information can be used by web browsers to display information about the page and can aid in search engine optimization.

The name attribute specifies the type of metadata provided, while the content attribute contains the actual metadata content.

The charset attribute specifies the web page's character encoding.

Items.Title: 为什么状态管理对 React 应用很重要？ | Why is State Management Important For React Apps?
Items.Description:
企业主必须做出的最重要和最困难的决定是如何构建他们的企业应用程序，使其易于维护、可重用提供高性能，最重要的是，该应用程序必须具有良好的可扩展性。

如果您拥有 React.js 企业应用程序，那么透彻了解React 状态管理至关重要。 这是 Web 开发领域中一个引人入胜的主题。 既然我们了解了开发业务应用程序所涉及的挑战，让我们看看状态管理 React 库如何帮助您的公司应用程序实现您的业务目标。

你会想要利用应用程序中组件的状态，因为 React Js 应用程序提供速度、自由、丰富的用户界面等等。

探索实现 React 状态管理的各种方法：

组件状态

每个 React 组件都有一个内部状态，可用于管理和保存特定于组件的数据。 此方法更新组件的状态并导致重新渲染，用于处理此状态。

上下文API

当元素树中未直接链接的功能需要共享状态时，这可以替代使用组成状态。

创建模型

要在元素之间共享状态，您可以使用 render props 模式将函数作为 prop 传输，以呈现需要它的功能。 此方法可用于在元素树中未高度嵌套的段之间共享状态。

反应挂钩

功能组件可以使用 React Hooks 添加状态和其他 React 功能。 挂钩可用于在本地处理本地组件状态，而其他挂钩可用于从上下文 API 检索共享状态。

这些著名的库是现成的，用于管理 React JS 应用程序中的状态，每个React js 开发公司都必须提供：

这个基于 Redux 的 React 应用程序库是由 Shawn McKay 创建的。 它为开发 Redux 存储和缩减程序提供了一个简化的 API，减少了对代码的需求并提高了开发人员的产出。

这个用于 React 应用程序路由的库是由 Ryan Florence 和 Michael Jackson 创建的。 对于更小或更简单的应用程序，它可以作为 Redux 和 MobX 的不太复杂的替代品。

为了以可预测性、模块化和可测试性为重点来处理程序的状态，David Khourshid 开发了这个库。

该库由 Adob​​e 创建，其核心是提供许多可用于处理复杂 UI 状态的挂钩和组件，例如表单输入、下拉菜单和菜单中的状态。

这个 React 应用程序的可重用钩子集合是由 Fabien Juif 创建的。 结论

要创建可扩展、有效且可靠的 React 应用程序，企业家必须在React 状态管理方面做出最关键的选择。 它保证我们的程序跟上用户界面。 为了在 React 应用程序中处理和管理状态，我们看到了各种内置和第三方选择。 您从众多选项中做出的决定受项目需求和开发团队人数的影响。



原文:

The most important and difficult decision a business owner must make is how to construct their enterprise app so that it is simple to maintain, reusable provides high performance, and most importantly, the app must have a good scope of scalability.

A thorough understanding of React State Management is crucial if you have a React.js enterprise app. It is a fascinating subject in the web development field. Let's look at how state management React libraries can help your corporate app meet your business objectives now that we know the challenges involved in developing business apps.

Various Methods of State Handling in React

You would want to take advantage of the state of components in your app because React Js apps offer speed, freedom, rich user interfaces, and so much more.

Discover the various approaches to achieving React state management:

Component State

React components each have an internal state that can be used to manage and keep component-specific data. This method updates the component's state and causes a re-render, is used to handle this state.

Contextual API

React has built-in functionality that makes it possible to share state between elements without using props to pass data down the element order. This can be a useful substitute for using a constituent state when features that are not directly linked in the element tree need to share a state.

Create Models

To share the state between elements, you can use the render props pattern to transmit a function as a prop that renders the feature that needs it. This method may be used to share state between segments that aren't highly nested in the element tree.

React Hooks

Functional components can add state and other React capabilities using React Hooks. The hooks can be used to handle local component state locally, whereas other hook can be used to retrieve shared state from the Context API.

The Best Libraries for React State Management

These well-known libraries are ready-made for managing states in React JS apps that every React js development company must provide:

1. Rematch

This Redux-based library for React apps was created by Shawn McKay. It offers a streamlined API for developing Redux stores and reducers, reducing the need for code and boosting developer output.

2. Valtio

With an emphasis on performance and developer productivity, Valtio Poimandres created the Valtio library, which employs a minimalistic and reactive strategy to manage the state of an app.

3. React-router

This library for routing in React apps was created by Ryan Florence and Michael Jackson. React Router offers a declarative method for handling routing in a React app with an emphasis on efficiency, freedom, and simplicity.

4. Jotai

With a focus on efficiency and performance, Pedro Nauck's Jotai manages an app's state using atoms and setters.

5. Unstated

Unstated is a compact library that allows components to exchange state using the React context API. For smaller or simpler apps, it serves as a less complex substitute for Redux and MobX.

6. XState

To handle the state of a program with a focus on predictability, modularity, and testability, David Khourshid developed this library.

7. Hookstate

Rafael Wieland recently created the tool Hookstate for managing React state. At its heart, Hookstate emphasizes performance and developer productivity while using a streamlined approach to manage an app's state.

8. Zustand

A simplified method is used by Guillaume Salva's Zustand state management library for React to handle an app's state.

9. React-stately

This library was created by Adobe, and at its core, it offers many hooks and components that can be used to handle complex UI states, like those present in form inputs, dropdown menus, and menus.

10. React-powerhooks

This collection of reusable hooks for React apps was created by Fabien Juif. React Powerhooks' core functionality is a collection of hooks for controlling common UI states like loading, error handling, and form validation.

Conclusion

To create scalable, effective, and reliable React apps, entrepreneurs must make the most crucial choice regarding react state management. It guarantees that our program keeps up with the user interface. To handle and manage states in a React app, we saw the various built-in as well as third-party choices. Your decision from among the many options is influenced by the needs of your project and the number of your development team.

Items.Title: 基于 gpt-3.5-turbo 模型 | Python + ChatGPT API Development | Based on gpt-3.5-turbo model
Items.Description:
全新的GPT-3.5-turbo模型是在GPT-3模型基础上的升级改进，具有更高的准确率和表现力，能够自动识别文本的语义和上下文，生成更准确、更自然的回复。

聊天软件

聊天GPT API

介绍

之前写过一篇Develop an Intelligent Chat Program Using Python and ChatGPT API的文章，介绍了使用text-davinci-002 （或text-davinci-003 ）模型作为Python开发的例子，这次ChatGPT官方更新了gpt-3.5-turbo模型，在接口上会有一些调整，提供更多的API参数和选项，方便开发者根据自己的需求定制和优化API的性能。

如果使用新模型，价格为每 1000 个代币 0.002 美元，比当前的GPT-3.5模型便宜 10 倍。 具有高可用性和可扩展性，也可以应对高流量的应用场景。 官方测试工程师从text-davinci-003迁移到gpt-3.5-turbo ，只需要少量调整即可完成开发，并提供非常友好的开发文档和示例代码，帮助开发者快速上手。

用法

如果您只是想快速验证 ChatGPT API 是否可用，您可以尝试使用curl发送请求。

 curl https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d'{ "model": "gpt-3.5-turbo", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is the OpenAI mission?"}] }'

如果你需要深入ChatGPT API的开发，我将向你展示如何使用Python + ChatGPT API快速构建基于ChatGPT的软件。

这是基本用法的代码示例：

 # Pay attention to check that your OpenAI version must be v0.27.0 or above import openai # First, you need to set up your API key openai.api_key = "YOUR_API_KEY" # Then, you can call the "gpt-3.5-turbo" model model_engine = "gpt-3.5-turbo" # set your input text input_text = "Where is the 2014 World Cup held?" # Send an API request and get a response, note that the interface and parameters have changed compared to the old model response = openai.ChatCompletion.create( model=model_engine, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": input_text }] ) # response will get a json message with a structure like this # { # 'id': 'chatcmpl-6p9XYPYSTTRi0xEviKjjilqrWU2Ve', # 'object': 'chat.completion', # 'created': 1677649420, # 'model': 'gpt-3.5-turbo', # 'usage': {'prompt_tokens': 56, 'completion_tokens': 31, 'total_tokens': 87}, # 'choices': [ # { # 'message': { # 'role': 'assistant', # 'content': 'The 2014 FIFA World Cup was held in Brazil.'}, # 'finish_reason': 'stop', # 'index': 0 # } # ] # } # Parse the response and output the result output_text = response['choices'][0]['message']['content'] print("ChatGPT API reply:", output_text)

在上面的代码中，我们首先设置我们的 API 密钥，然后指定要使用的GPT-3.5-turbo模型，设置输入文本并发送 API 请求，最后解析响应并输出 ChatGPT 回复。 有一些注意事项

新接口：原来使用text-davinci-002模型，我们使用openai.Completion.create接口，现在新的gpt-3.5-turbo应该使用openai.ChatCompletion.create接口。

新增参数：第一个参数设置model模型名称，第二个参数为会话列表

为什么要提供对话列表？ 因为API调用是单接口请求，不会自动记录之前的聊天信息，没有上下文。 为了让 ChatGPT 在一次请求中了解你的上下文，你需要提供这样一个完整的对话列表，比如这个对话

import openai openai.ChatCompletion.create( model="gpt-3.5-turbo", messages=[ {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Who won the world series in 2020?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "The Los Angeles Dodgers won the World Series in 2020."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Where was it played?"} ] )

每个对话消息都需要提供角色和内容。 一共有三个角色： system ， user或assistant 。

  • 在上面的示例中，助手表示You are a helpful assistant 。
  • 一些参考ChatGPT 提示
  • 你也可以修改这个回复或者自己编一段对话，让整个对话更流畅。

如果您不需要对话框，只需提供一条user消息，如之前的 Python 代码所示。

更多开发文档请参考Chat补全

耳语API

它提供了一个large-v2模型，易于使用，价格为0.006美元/分钟，可以按需访问，高度优化的服务堆栈可以确保更快的性能。

一个简单的卷曲示例

curl https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" \ -H "Content-Type: multipart/form-data" \ -F model="whisper-1" \ -F file="@/path/to/file/openai.mp3"

接口响应格式

{ "text": "Imagine the wildest idea that you've ever had, and you're curious about how it might scale to something that's a 100, a 1,000 times bigger..." }

在 Python 中使用

import openai file = open("/path/to/file/openai.mp3", "rb") transcription = openai.Audio.transcribe("whisper-1", f) print(transcription)

更多开发文档请参考Speech to text

结论

以上只是一个简单的例子，展示了如何使用 Python 通过 ChatGPT API 发送请求，您可以根据需要使用 ChatGPT API 来完成更复杂的任务。

参考



原文:

ChatGPT is a cloud-based artificial intelligence chatbot that uses OpenAI's GPT-3.5-turbo model to implement natural language processing (NLP) and language generation tasks. The new GPT-3.5-turbo model is an upgrade and improvement based on the GPT-3 model, with higher accuracy and expressiveness, it can automatically identify the semantics and context of text, and generate more accurate and Natural reply.

The OpenAI team released the latest intelligent chatbot ChatGPT API and voice-to-text Whisper API. ChatGPT API provides a convenient and easy-to-use way for developers to use this powerful NLP model easily. The latest release of the ChatGPT API uses the model family gpt-3.5-turbo which is the same model used in the ChatGPT product.

chatopenai.pro

ChatGPT API

Introduction

I wrote an article about Develop an Intelligent Chat Program Using Python and ChatGPT API before, introducing the use of text-davinci-002 (or text-davinci-003) model as an example of Python development, this time ChatGPT officially updated the gpt-3.5-turbo model, there will be some adjustments on the interface, providing more API parameters And options, so that developers can customize and optimize the performance of the API according to their own needs.

If the new model is used, the price is $0.002 per 1000 tokens, which is 10 times cheaper than the current GPT-3.5 model. It has high availability and scalability, and can also handle high-traffic application scenarios. The official test engineer migrated from text-davinci-003 to gpt-3.5-turbo, only need a few adjustments to complete the development, and provides very friendly development documents and sample codes to help developers get started quickly.

Usage

If you just want to quickly verify that the ChatGPT API is available, you can try using curl to send the request.

curl https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d'{ "model": "gpt-3.5-turbo", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "What is the OpenAI mission?"}]
}'

If you need to go deep into the development of ChatGPT API, I will show you how to use Python + ChatGPT API to quickly build software based on ChatGPT.

Here is a code sample for basic usage:

# Pay attention to check that your OpenAI version must be v0.27.0 or above
import openai # First, you need to set up your API key
openai.api_key = "YOUR_API_KEY" # Then, you can call the "gpt-3.5-turbo" model
model_engine = "gpt-3.5-turbo" # set your input text
input_text = "Where is the 2014 World Cup held?" # Send an API request and get a response, note that the interface and parameters have changed compared to the old model
response = openai.ChatCompletion.create( model=model_engine, messages=[{"role": "user", "content": input_text }]
) # response will get a json message with a structure like this
# {
# 'id': 'chatcmpl-6p9XYPYSTTRi0xEviKjjilqrWU2Ve',
# 'object': 'chat.completion',
# 'created': 1677649420,
# 'model': 'gpt-3.5-turbo',
# 'usage': {'prompt_tokens': 56, 'completion_tokens': 31, 'total_tokens': 87},
# 'choices': [
# {
# 'message': {
# 'role': 'assistant',
# 'content': 'The 2014 FIFA World Cup was held in Brazil.'},
# 'finish_reason': 'stop',
# 'index': 0
# }
# ]
# }

# Parse the response and output the result
output_text = response['choices'][0]['message']['content']
print("ChatGPT API reply:", output_text)

In the code above, we first set up our API key, then specified the GPT-3.5-turbo model to use, set the input text and sent the API request, and finally parsed the response and output the ChatGPT reply . There are a few caveats

New interface: Originally used text-davinci-002 model, we used openai.Completion.create interface, now the new gpt-3.5-turbo should use openai.ChatCompletion.create interface.

New parameters: The first parameter sets the model model name, the second parameter is a conversation list

Why provide a conversation list? Because the API call is a single interface request, the previous chat information will not be automatically recorded, and there is no context. To let ChatGPT understand your context in a single request, you need to provide such a complete list of conversations, such as this one dialogue

import openai openai.ChatCompletion.create( model="gpt-3.5-turbo", messages=[ {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Who won the world series in 2020?"}, {"role": "assistant", "content": "The Los Angeles Dodgers won the World Series in 2020."}, {"role": "user", "content": "Where was it played?"} ]
)

Each dialog message needs to provide role and content. There are three roles: system, user or assistant.

  • system: The system message is equivalent to an administrator, who can set the behavior and characteristics of the assistant. In the example above, the assistant is indicated You are a helpful assistant.
  • user: The user message is ourselves, which can be asked by the user, or directly let the developer build some prompts in advance. Some reference ChatGPT Prompts
  • assistant: The assistant message is the reply provided by the ChatGPT API before, and it is stored here. You can also modify this reply or make up a dialogue yourself to make the whole dialogue more smooth.

If you don't need a dialog, just provide a single user message, as demonstrated in the Python code before.

For more development documents, please refer to Chat completions

Whisper API

OpenAI also released the Whisper API that supports speech-to-text. It offers a large-v2 model, which is easy to use, priced at $0.006/minute, can be accessed on demand, and a highly optimized service stack can ensure faster performance.

Whisper API is available through transcriptions (transcribes in source language) or translations (transcribes into English) endpoints, and accepts a variety of formats (m4a, mp3, mp4, mpeg, mpga, wav, webm):

A simple curl example

curl https://api.openai.com/v1/audio/transcriptions \ -H "Authorization: Bearer $OPENAI_API_KEY" \ -H "Content-Type: multipart/form-data" \ -F model="whisper-1" \ -F file="@/path/to/file/openai.mp3"

Interface response format

{ "text": "Imagine the wildest idea that you've ever had, and you're curious about how it might scale to something that's a 100, a 1,000 times bigger..."
}

Use in Python

import openai file = open("/path/to/file/openai.mp3", "rb")
transcription = openai.Audio.transcribe("whisper-1", f) print(transcription)

For more development documents, please refer to Speech to text

Conclusion

The above is just a simple example showing how to use Python to send requests with ChatGPT API, you can use ChatGPT API to complete more complex tasks according to your needs.

Expo is a popular open-source toolchain for developing and building mobile applications using React Native. It provides a set of pre-built native modules and components that makes it easy to create high-quality apps for Android and IOS.

In this blog, we will discuss Expo Application Services (EAS) and the usage of eas-cli for creating builds for IOS and Android.

Expo Application Services (EAS)

EAS is a set of cloud-based tools and services that provide end-to-end support for building, deploying and managing Expo applications. It includes a wide range of features such as over-the-air updates, push notifications, analytics, and more.

EAS is available for free, with some limitations on usage, and also offers a paid plan for business and teams that require more resources and features.

One of the key benefits of using Expo Application Services is the ability to build and deploy applications without requiring any native build tools or infrastructure. Expo provides a cloud-based service that compiles your JavaScript code and assets into native code for IOS and Android, allowing you to quickly test and deploy your app to real devices. Expo also provides over-the-updates, which allow you to make changes to your app without requiring users to download a new version from the App Store / Play Store.

EAS-CLI

Expo Application Services CLI (eas-cli) is a command-line interface tool that provides a set of commands for managing and deploying Expo applications. EAS-CLI, is a replacement for Expo's legacy build tools (expo-cli) and provides a more streamlined and efficient workflow.

Installation

To use EAS-CLI, you first need to install it using npm. Open your terminal or command prompt and enter the following command:

EAS Login

Once EAS-CLI is installed, you need to login/signup for with Expo Account using the following command:

If you are already logged in using Expo Account using Expo-CLI, there is no need to log in again. You can check whether you are logged in or not by using the following command:

Configuration for EAS Build

To configure your application for EAS Build, run the following command:

This will create an eas.json file in your project which will have information about your cli version and different profile like development, preview, production, etc.

Building

The profiles can be modified according to your preference. Let's assume you want to build an APK file to test on real Android devices for testing purposes under the development profile, you can achieve that by adding the following tags in your eas.json:

'android.buildType': 'apk' ,
'gradleCommand': ':app:assembleRelease'

In order to specifically build the development profile you can run the following command:

eas build -p android --profile development

where, -p stands for platform.

You can get more information on building for Android and IOS from the embedded links.

Building binaries for App Store and Play Store

You can build the binary for Android and IOS using the following EAS-CLI commands:

eas build --platform ios
eas build --pltform android

EAS-CLI also provides a range of other commands for managing your build configuration, credentials, and more. You can run the following command to view the status of your builds:

This will show you a list of your recent builds, along with their status, duration, and other details.

In conclusion, Expo Application Services and EAS-CLI provides a powerful and efficient workflow for building, deploying, and managing Expo applications. With EAS, you can quickly build and deploy your app without requiring any native build tools or infrastructure. EAS-CLI enables you to manage your build configuration and credentials from the command line, making it easier to automate your build and deployment process.

如果您想知道升级到 Oracle EBS 12.2 有什么好处，那么这篇博客适合您。 在本文中，我们将讨论为什么应该升级到 EBS 12.2、与此相关的好处是什么，以及如何正确地做到这一点。

将 Oracle EBS 升级到 R12.2 的主要好处

高级支持：如果您是现有的 Oracle EBS 客户，您必须知道 Oracle 在 EBS R12.1 之前已停止提供高级支持。 这意味着即使您已经支付了许可费用中的支持费用，您也不会获得重要的补丁更新、安全警报、数据修复以及与新税收或法规要求相关的更新。 此外，Oracle 不会对新的第三方产品和版本进行认证。 否则，您将不再保持顺从。 但是，将 Oracle EBS 升级到 R12.2 将使您有资格再享受 10 年的首要支持。 持续创新：甲骨文遵循“持续创新”的原则。 这意味着一旦您使用 EBS 12.2，就不需要任何重大升级。 但是，Oracle 将继续提供“关键补丁单元”并添加特性和功能以帮助您有效地管理您的业务。

在线修补：以前，修补会导致严重的停机时间。 因此，EBS 客户需要尽快实施补丁以减少停机时间。 通过升级到 EBS 12.2，您不必担心任何停机时间，因为可以在系统运行和完全运行时应用补丁。 直观的用户体验：EBS 12.2 具有全新的外观和感觉，可提供增强的用户体验。 用户将收到通知警报并可以调整小部件的宽度。 为了提高生产力，EBS 12.2 还支持移动性。 用户可以借助智能手机访问公司数据。

由于您现在已经了解将 Oracle EBS 升级到 12.2 的好处，因此您需要了解升级到 EBS 12.2 是您需要执行的最后一个困难升级。 对于无风险的 EBS 升级，您需要遵循以下提示。

在 EBS 升级期间，您需要评估功能以及 CEMLI 以了解对开发人员的依赖性。 这也为您提供了项目的范围。 但是，需要保护定制在升级过程中不被覆盖。 为确保这一点，需要进行大量测试以检查表或 API 中的更改是否未影响现有的自定义设置。

为此，需要改造测试脚本，并编写额外的脚本来检查业务流程的偏差。

正如您现在了解的 EBS 升级最佳实践，您必须了解升级期间的重要测试。 由于需要多轮测试，不用说您应该坚持手动测试方法。 手动测试不仅耗时且容易出错，而且还存在不足的风险。

为确保您的 EBS 升级保持无风险且在时限内，您需要引入测试自动化。 如果您想知道哪些是合适的 Oracle EBS 测试工具，市场上有许多付费和开源工具。 然而，在为 Oracle EBS 选择测试自动化工具时，请记住业务用户在成功升级中起着关键作用。 他们是非技术人员。 因此，如果您引入一个只需最少培训即可操作的零代码测试自动化平台，那将是一件好事。



原文:


If you’re wondering what are the benefits of upgrading to Oracle EBS 12.2, this blog is for you. In this article, we’ll discuss why you should upgrade to EBS 12.2, what are the benefits associated with this, and how to do this right.

Top benefits of upgrading Oracle EBS to R12.2

Premier Support: If you’re an existing Oracle EBS customer, you must be aware that Oracle has stopped offering premier support till EBS R12.1. It means that even though you’ve paid for support in the licensing fee, you’ll not get critical patch updates, security alerts, data fixes, and updates related to new tax or regulatory requirements. Furthermore, Oracle will not certify new third-party products and versions. Saying it otherwise, you’ll no longer remain compliant. However, upgrading Oracle EBS to R12.2 will make you eligible for premier support for another 10 years. Oracle has announced that it will keep on providing support to EBS R12.2 version till 2031.

Continuous Innovation: Oracle works on the principle of “Continuous Innovation”. It means that once you’re on EBS 12.2, you don’t need any major upgrade. However, Oracle will keep on delivering “critical patch units” and adding features and functionalities to help you efficiently manage your business.

Online Patching: Previously, patching led to serious downtime. So, EBS customers were required to implement patches as quickly as possible to reduce downtime. By upgrading to EBS 12.2, you don’t have to worry about any downtime since patches can be applied while your system is running and fully operational. EBS R12.2 upgrade significantly reduces maintenance downtime.

Intuitive User Experience: EBS 12.2 comes in a new look and feel to offer enhanced user experience. Users will get a notification alert and can adjust widths of widgets. To improve productivity, EBS 12.2 also supports mobility. Users can have access to corporate data with the help of smartphones.

As you’re now aware about the benefits of upgrading Oracle EBS to 12.2, you need to understand that upgrading to EBS 12.2 is the last difficult upgrade you need to perform. For risk-free EBS upgrade, you need to follow the given below tips.

● CEMLI Evaluation: CEMLI stands for configurations/ customization, extension, modification, localization and internationalization, and integration. During EBS upgrade, you need to evaluate the functionality as well as CEMLI to understand the dependencies on the developers. This also provides you with the scope of the project. However, customizations need to be protected from being overwritten during the upgrade process. To ensure this, extensive testing needs to be done to check whether the changes in tables or APIs haven’t impacted the existing customizations.

● CEMLI Retrofitment: As there’s a possibility that some of the customizations need to be replaced with the standard functionality, you need to test all the CEMLI to make sure that standard features and functionalities are satisfying the business requirements that were previously met with the custom code.

● Upgrade testing: Test scripts that were used initially during EBS implementation needs to be used again as baseline to check the functionality of the apps post upgrade. For this, test scripts need to be retrofitted and additional scripts need to be written to check business process deviations.

● UAT: It is the final round of testing that needs to be performed by end users to check whether or not the upgraded system is meeting up their expectations and helping them in doing day-to-day working.

As you now know best practices for EBS upgrade, you must be aware of the important testing during upgrade. As multiple rounds of testing is required, it is needless to say that you should stick to manual testing approaches. Manual testing is not only time consuming and error prone but also plagued with inadequate risks.

To ensure that your EBS upgrade remains risk-free and within timelines, you need to bring in test automation. If you’re wondering which are the rightful Oracle EBS testing tools for this, there are many paid and open source tools available in the market. However, while selecting test automation tools for Oracle EBS, keep in mind that business users play a critical role in successful upgrades. They are non-technical persons. So, it would be good if you bring in a zero code test automation platform that can be operated with minimum training.

The customer have clear details of what the product is, the cost and can also go further to purchase it.

Let’s have a look at how we can build such an amazing component.

It should be noted that this tutorial is done using TailwindCSS, which works using Node Package Manager (npm), so it is important that Node is installed, and then tailwindcss should be install later on. You can read more at tailwindcss.com/docs/installation

 <div>

 </div>

All the different properties use here are pretty self-explanatory, but nonetheless, let’s understand them together.

  • text-white bg-black rounded-full w-fit px-3 : Simply put, background-color: black, color: white (text color), border-radius: 100%, width: fit-content and padding-inline: 0.75rem. This properties were all applied to have the Free Shipping component at the top of the cart (yeah ! that lil beautiful thing saying free shipping)

  • Line-through: As you might have guess, it simply put a line through on the text on which it is apply on.

let’s have a look on the second sub-section.

            </div>

            <div>
                <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="1em" height="1em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><path fill="currentColor" d="M128 224a7.8 7.8 0 0 1-3.9-1C119.8 220.6 20 163.9 20 92a60 60 0 0 1 108-36a60 60 0 0 1 108 36c0 30.6-17.7 62-52.6 93.4a314.3 314.3 0 0 1-51.5 37.6a7.8 7.8 0 0 1-3.9 1Zm-3.9-15ZM80 48a44 44 0 0 0-44 44c0 55.2 74 103.7 92 114.7c18-11 92-59.5 92-114.7a44 44 0 0 0-84.6-17a8 8 0 0 1-14.8 0A43.8 43.8 0 0 0 80 48Z"/></svg>
            </div>
            <h2>Add to wishlist</h2></div>
    </div>
</div>

This sub-section mainly contain all the different call to actions buttons like :** Add to Cart** and Add to Wishlist

You can get all the icons used in this tutorial here

The properties [&>*] simply means “select each individual child”, this allows us to apply the same styling properties to all the immediate children

And That’s it ! All should be good here! 😎

Let’s focus on the main component now, and see how we can style the background. . . ✍️

Back to the main containers, Let’s make this a flex box and give some cool styling too.

<body class="bg-blue-100 flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen">

 </div>

  • bg-blue-100 flex items-center justify-center min-h-screen : This properties ensures that our cart is exactly at the center of the screen (display: flex align-items: center justify-content: center min-height: 100vh)

  • flex bg-white rounded-lg w-fit p-10 mx-auto drop-shadow-2xl : This makes our cart item container a flex box with a fit-content width and a drop-shadow.

And we did it ! 😍

Conclusion

We just built a simple Cart item component without opening our CSS file😃. Thanks to Tailwindcss. Many employers will need such components to be added to their website, and right now you should be proud that you are one those few who knows how to build it in less than 5mins, and you do that without even leaving you HTML document 😎.

You can have a Live preview on Codepen or find the code on Github

Don’t hesitate to share with me if you were able to complete the tutorial on your end, I’d be happy to see any additions styling you added to you cart item.

Items.Title: UiPath 机器人：类型和应用 | UiPath Robots: Types and Applications
Items.Description:
普里亚沙玛

发表于 3 月 2 日

在本文中，我们将探讨不同类型的 UiPath 机器人、它们的功能及其应用。

有人值守机器人是与人类工作者一起工作、协助完成任务并提供支持的 UiPath 机器人。 这些机器人设计为在人类工作人员的直接监督下运行，通常由用户的动作或请求触发。 有人值守机器人对于需要人工监督或决策的任务特别有用，因为它们可以帮助减少错误、提高效率，并腾出时间让人类工人专注于更复杂的任务。

有人值守机器人的特点

有人值守机器人有几个特性，使其成为与人类工作者一起工作的理想选择，包括：

  1. 用户触发：有人值守机器人通常由用户的操作触发，例如单击按钮或发送请求。

  2. 人类监督：有人值守的机器人在人类工人的直接监督下运行，人类工人可以监视和控制他们的行为。

  3. 用户界面：有人值守的机器人通常有一个图形用户界面 (GUI)，允许用户与其交互并提供输入。

用例和应用程序

有人值守机器人非常适合各种任务和应用，包括：

  1. 数据输入：有人值守机器人可以根据用户输入自动填充字段或从其他来源提取数据，从而协助完成数据输入任务。
  2. 客户服务：Attended Robots 可以通过提供对客户信息的快速访问、协助处理常见查询以及将请求路由到适当的部门来帮助客户服务代表。
  3. 文件处理：有人值守机器人可以协助完成文件处理任务，例如整理、归档和归档文件。
  4. 销售支持： Attended Robots 可以通过提供对客户数据的访问、跟踪销售活动和自动执行例行任务来帮助销售代表。
  5. 质量控制：有人值守机器人可以协助执行质量控制任务，例如检查文档是否存在错误或不一致之处。

有人值守机器人几乎可用于任何行业或部门，其灵活性和易用性使其成为寻求提高效率和降低成本的组织的宝贵工具。 通过与人类工作者一起工作，有人值守机器人可以帮助简化流程、提高准确性并腾出时间来执行更具战略意义的任务。

无人值守机器人是无需人工干预即可运行的 UiPath 机器人，可按计划或基于特定触发器执行自动化任务。 这些机器人旨在自主工作，使用预定义的工作流程和业务规则来执行日常任务和流程。 无人值守机器人对于重复性、耗时或需要 24/7 运行的任务特别有用。

无人值守机器人的特点
无人值守机器人具有多项功能，使其成为自动化任务的理想选择，包括：

  1. 计划运行：无人值守机器人可以编程为按计划运行，例如在下班时间或周末运行。

  2. 基于触发器的操作：无人值守机器人也可以由特定事件触发，例如电子邮件到达或任务完成。

  3. 无需人工干预：无人值守机器人不需要人工干预或监督，可以自主运行。

用例和应用程序

无人值守机器人非常适合各种任务和应用，包括：

  1. 批处理：无人值守机器人可以执行批处理任务，例如数据输入、报告生成和发票处理，而无需人工干预。
  2. 系统监控：无人值守机器人可以监控系统和应用程序的错误、警报或其他事件，从而实现主动维护和问题解决。
  3. 数据管理：无人值守机器人可以管理和操作大量数据，例如数据提取、清洗和迁移。
  4. 财务和会计：无人值守机器人可以自动执行诸如账户核对、费用报告和发票审批等任务。
  5. 医疗保健：无人值守机器人可以自动执行与医疗保健相关的任务，例如患者记录管理、索赔处理和预约安排。

无人值守机器人几乎可以用于任何行业或部门，它们的自主操作能力使它们成为希望提高效率、降低成本和提高准确性的组织的宝贵工具。 通过自动化日常任务和流程，无人值守机器人可以让人类工人腾出时间专注于更具战略性的任务，同时还提供 24/7 全天候运营和更高的一致性。

混合机器人是结合了有人值守和无人值守机器人功能的 UiPath 机器人。 这些机器人可以在有或没有人为干预的情况下运行，具体取决于正在执行的特定任务或过程。 混合机器人被设计为灵活且适应性强，允许它们根据需要在有人值守和无人值守模式之间切换。

混合动力机器人的特点

混合机器人具有多项功能，使其成为各种任务的理想选择，包括：

  1. 在模式之间切换的能力：混合机器人可以根据正在执行的特定任务或过程在有人值守和无人值守模式之间切换。

  2. 需要时进行人为干预：混合机器人可以在必要时提示人类工作者进行输入或决策，从而提高准确性和灵活性。

  3. 基于计划或基于触发器的操作：混合机器人可以编程为按计划操作或由特定事件触发，具体取决于组织的需要。

用例和应用程序
混合机器人非常适合各种任务和应用，包括：

  1. 客户服务： Hybrid Robots 可以提供客户支持和帮助，例如回答常见问题或帮助客户浏览网站或应用程序。
  2. 销售和营销：混合机器人可以执行潜在客户生成、客户分析和活动管理等任务。
  3. 人力资源和招聘：混合机器人可以自动执行简历筛选、候选人外展和入职等任务。
  4. 财务和会计：混合机器人可以执行账户对账、费用报告和发票审批等任务。

混合机器人是希望最大化自动化同时保持高度灵活性和适应性的组织的理想解决方案。 通过结合有人值守和无人值守机器人的功能，混合机器人可以为组织提供两全其美的服务，在可能的情况下实现自动化，同时在必要时仍提供人工监督和干预。

使用 UiPath 机器人实现楼宇自动化

然而，构建有效且高效的自动化项目需要仔细规划和执行。 在本节中，我们将探索使用 UiPath 机器人设计和构建自动化项目的一些最佳实践，以及最大限度提高其效率和有效性的技巧。

设计和构建自动化项目的最佳实践

确定要自动化的正确任务和流程：在构建自动化项目之前，确定最适合自动化的任务和流程非常重要。 寻找重复的、基于规则的、耗时或容易出错的任务。

  • 从小处着手并迭代：与其尝试一次自动化整个流程，不如从一小部分任务开始并逐步构建。 这允许在整个过程中进行测试和改进，从而导致更有效和高效的自动化项目。

  • 使用合适的 UiPath 机器人完成工作：选择最适合特定任务或自动化流程的 UiPath 机器人类型（有人值守、无人值守或混合）。

  • 可扩展性和可维护性设计：在构建自动化项目时考虑可扩展性和可维护性。 这意味着设计的项目可以随着业务的发展轻松更新和修改，而无需进行大量返工。

  • 确保安全性和合规性：在设计和构建自动化项目时，一定要考虑安全性和合规性要求。 这包括确保敏感数据受到保护以及自动化项目符合相关法规。

最大化效率和有效性的技巧

  • 优化自动化工作流程：通过删除不必要的步骤和优化系统之间的数据流来简化自动化工作流程。

  • 使用 UiPath Orchestrator： UiPath Orchestrator 是一款用于管理和监控 UiPath 机器人的强大工具。 使用它来安排作业、监控性能和解决问题。

  • 监控和分析性能：使用分析工具监控 UiPath 机器人的性能并确定需要改进的地方。

  • 实施错误处理和恢复：构建具有强大错误处理和恢复机制的自动化项目，以确保即使在出现错误或异常时流程也能继续平稳运行。

  • 不断改进和优化：最后，随着时间的推移不断改进和优化自动化项目也很重要。 这意味着收集反馈、分析性能数据并根据需要进行调整，以确保自动化尽可能有效和高效。

通过遵循这些最佳实践和技巧，组织可以构建既有效又高效的自动化项目，并为业务带来真正的价值。

在本节中，我们将探讨 UiPath Robots 如何与其他系统集成，以及 UiPath 的集成功能。

将 UiPath 机器人与其他系统集成

企业资源规划 (ERP) 系统： UiPath 机器人可以与 SAP、Oracle 和 Microsoft Dynamics 等 ERP 系统集成，以自动执行数据输入、发票处理和库存管理等任务。

  • 客户关系管理 (CRM) 系统： UiPath 机器人可以与 Salesforce、HubSpot 和 Microsoft Dynamics CRM 等 CRM 系统集成，以自动执行潜在客户生成、数据输入和客户支持等任务。

  • 人力资源 (HR) 系统： UiPath 机器人可以与 Workday、SAP SuccessFactors 和 ADP 等人力资源系统集成，以自动执行员工入职、绩效管理和福利管理等任务。

  • 文档管理系统： UiPath Robots 可以与 SharePoint、Google Drive 和 Box 等文档管理系统集成，以自动执行文档处理、归档和检索等任务。

    • 这些能力包括：

    • 连接器和集成：UiPath 为流行的系统和平台提供各种预构建的连接器和集成，包括 ERP、CRM 和 HR 系统，以及文档管理系统和消息传递平台。

    • 通过利用 UiPath 的集成功能，组织可以轻松地将 UiPath 机器人与其他系统和平台集成，并构建端到端的自动化解决方案，为业务带来真正的价值。

    结论

    总之，UiPath Robots 提供了一个强大而灵活的解决方案，用于自动化广泛的任务和流程。 通过利用不同类型的 UiPath 机器人，组织可以自动执行从简单和重复到复杂和关键任务的任务。 此外，UiPath 机器人可以与其他系统和平台集成，使其成为端到端自动化解决方案的重要组成部分。
    对于希望在自动化领域建立职业生涯的个人，UiPath 认证提供了一个全面的计划，提供成为 UiPath 机器人专家所需的技能和知识。 通过UiPath 认证，个人可以向潜在雇主和客户展示他们的专业知识和信誉。

    原文:

    priya sharma

    Posted on Mar 2

    Brief overview of UiPath Robots.

    UiPath is one of the leading providers of robotic process automation (RPA) solutions, with a range of tools and technologies designed to help organizations automate routine tasks and streamline workflows. One of the key components of UiPath's RPA offering is the UiPath Robot, which comes in three different types: Attended Robots, Unattended Robots, and Hybrid Robots. In this article, we will explore the different types of UiPath Robots, their features, and their applications.

    1. Attended Robots

    Attended Robots are UiPath Robots that work alongside human workers, assisting with tasks and providing support. These robots are designed to operate under the direct supervision of a human worker and are typically triggered by the user's actions or requests. Attended Robots are particularly useful for tasks that require human oversight or decision-making, as they can help to reduce errors, improve efficiency, and free up time for human workers to focus on more complex tasks.

    Features of Attended Robots

    Attended Robots have several features that make them ideal for working alongside human workers, including:

    1. User-triggered: Attended Robots are typically triggered by the user's actions, such as clicking a button or sending a request.

    2. Human oversight: Attended Robots operate under the direct supervision of a human worker, who can monitor and control their actions.

    3. User interface: Attended Robots often have a graphical user interface (GUI) that allows users to interact with them and provide input.

    Use Cases and Applications

    Attended Robots are well-suited for a wide range of tasks and applications, including:

    1. Data entry: Attended Robots can assist with data entry tasks by automatically populating fields based on user input or by extracting data from other sources.
    2. Customer service: Attended Robots can help customer service representatives by providing quick access to customer information, assisting with common inquiries, and routing requests to the appropriate department.
    3. Document processing: Attended Robots can assist with document processing tasks, such as sorting, filing, and archiving documents.
    4. Sales support: Attended Robots can help sales representatives by providing access to customer data, tracking sales activities, and automating routine tasks.
    5. Quality control: Attended Robots can assist with quality control tasks, such as reviewing documents for errors or inconsistencies.

    Attended Robots can be used in almost any industry or sector, and their flexibility and ease of use make them a valuable tool for organizations looking to increase efficiency and reduce costs. By working alongside human workers, Attended Robots can help to streamline processes, improve accuracy, and free up time for more strategic tasks.

    2. Unattended Robots

    Unattended Robots are UiPath Robots that can operate without human intervention, performing automated tasks on a schedule or based on specific triggers. These robots are designed to work autonomously, using pre-defined workflows and business rules to carry out routine tasks and processes. Unattended Robots are particularly useful for tasks that are repetitive, time-consuming, or require 24/7 operation.

    Features of Unattended Robots
    Unattended Robots have several features that make them ideal for automated tasks, including:

    1. Scheduled operation: Unattended Robots can be programmed to run on a schedule, such as during off-hours or on weekends.

    2. Trigger-based operation: Unattended Robots can also be triggered by specific events, such as the arrival of an email or the completion of a task.

    3. No human intervention: Unattended Robots do not require human intervention or oversight, allowing them to operate autonomously.

    Use Cases and Applications

    Unattended Robots are well-suited for a wide range of tasks and applications, including:

    1. Batch processing: Unattended Robots can perform batch processing tasks, such as data entry, report generation, and invoice processing, without requiring human intervention.
    2. System monitoring: Unattended Robots can monitor systems and applications for errors, alerts, or other events, allowing for proactive maintenance and issue resolution.
    3. Data management: Unattended Robots can manage and manipulate large amounts of data, such as data extraction, cleansing, and migration.
    4. Finance and accounting: Unattended Robots can automate tasks such as account reconciliation, expense reporting, and invoice approval.
    5. Healthcare: Unattended Robots can automate healthcare-related tasks such as patient record management, claims processing, and appointment scheduling.

    Unattended Robots can be used in almost any industry or sector, and their ability to operate autonomously makes them a valuable tool for organizations looking to increase efficiency, reduce costs, and improve accuracy. By automating routine tasks and processes, Unattended Robots can free up human workers to focus on more strategic tasks, while also providing 24/7 operation and improved consistency.

    3. Hybrid Robots

    Hybrid Robots are UiPath Robots that combine the features of both Attended and Unattended Robots. These robots can operate with or without human intervention, depending on the specific task or process being performed. Hybrid Robots are designed to be flexible and adaptable, allowing them to switch between attended and unattended modes as needed.

    Features of Hybrid Robots

    Hybrid Robots have several features that make them ideal for a variety of tasks, including:

    1. Ability to switch between modes: Hybrid Robots can switch between attended and unattended modes, depending on the specific task or process being performed.

    2. Human intervention when required: Hybrid Robots can prompt human workers for input or decision-making when necessary, allowing for greater accuracy and flexibility.

    3. Schedule-based or trigger-based operation: Hybrid Robots can be programmed to operate on a schedule or triggered by specific events, depending on the needs of the organization.

    Use Cases and Applications
    Hybrid Robots are well-suited for a wide range of tasks and applications, including:

    1. Customer service: Hybrid Robots can provide customer support and assistance, such as answering frequently asked questions or helping customers navigate a website or application.
    2. Sales and marketing:Hybrid Robots can perform tasks such as lead generation, customer profiling, and campaign management.
    3. HR and recruiting: Hybrid Robots can automate tasks such as resume screening, candidate outreach, and onboarding.
    4. IT support: Hybrid Robots can provide support for IT-related tasks such as software installation, patching, and updates.
    5. Finance and accounting: Hybrid Robots can perform tasks such as account reconciliation, expense reporting, and invoice approval.

    Hybrid Robots are an ideal solution for organizations looking to maximize automation while also maintaining a high degree of flexibility and adaptability. By combining the features of Attended and Unattended Robots, Hybrid Robots can provide organizations with the best of both worlds, allowing for automation when possible, while still providing human oversight and intervention when necessary.

    Building Automation with UiPath Robots

    UiPath Robots are powerful tools for automating a wide range of tasks and processes. However, building effective and efficient automation projects requires careful planning and execution. In this section, we'll explore some best practices for designing and building automation projects using UiPath Robots, as well as tips for maximizing their efficiency and effectiveness.

    Best Practices for Designing and Building Automation Projects

    Identify the right tasks and processes to automate: Before building an automation project, it's important to identify the tasks and processes that are best suited for automation. Look for repetitive, rule-based tasks that are time-consuming or prone to errors.

    • Start small and iterate: Rather than attempting to automate an entire process at once, start with a small subset of tasks and build incrementally. This allows for testing and refinement along the way, leading to a more effective and efficient automation project.

    • Use the right UiPath Robot for the job: Choose the type of UiPath Robot (Attended, Unattended, or Hybrid) that is best suited for the specific task or process being automated.

    • Design for scalability and maintainability: Build automation projects with scalability and maintainability in mind. This means designing projects that can be easily updated and modified as the business evolves, without the need for significant rework.

    • Ensure security and compliance: When designing and building automation projects, be sure to consider security and compliance requirements. This includes ensuring that sensitive data is protected and that the automation project is in compliance with relevant regulations.

    Tips for Maximizing Efficiency and Effectiveness

    • Optimize automation workflows: Streamline automation workflows by removing unnecessary steps and optimizing the flow of data between systems.

    • Utilize UiPath Orchestrator: UiPath Orchestrator is a powerful tool for managing and monitoring UiPath Robots. Use it to schedule jobs, monitor performance, and troubleshoot issues.

    • Monitor and analyze performance: Use analytics tools to monitor the performance of UiPath Robots and identify areas for improvement.

    • Implement error handling and recovery: Build automation projects with robust error handling and recovery mechanisms to ensure that processes continue to run smoothly even in the event of errors or exceptions.

    • Continuously improve and optimize: Finally, it's important to continuously improve and optimize automation projects over time. This means collecting feedback, analyzing performance data, and making adjustments as needed to ensure that automation is as effective and efficient as possible.

    By following these best practices and tips, organizations can build automation projects that are both effective and efficient, and that deliver real value to the business.

    UiPath Robots and Integration

    One of the key strengths of UiPath Robots is their ability to integrate with a wide range of other systems and platforms. In this section, we'll explore how UiPath Robots can be integrated with other systems, as well as UiPath's integration capabilities.

    Integrating UiPath Robots with Other Systems

    UiPath Robots can be integrated with a variety of other systems and platforms, including:

    1. Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems: UiPath Robots can be integrated with ERP systems such as SAP, Oracle, and Microsoft Dynamics to automate tasks such as data entry, invoice processing, and inventory management.

    2. Customer Relationship Management (CRM) systems: UiPath Robots can be integrated with CRM systems such as Salesforce, HubSpot, and Microsoft Dynamics CRM to automate tasks such as lead generation, data entry, and customer support.

    3. Human Resources (HR) systems: UiPath Robots can be integrated with HR systems such as Workday, SAP SuccessFactors, and ADP to automate tasks such as employee onboarding, performance management, and benefits administration.

    4. Document Management systems: UiPath Robots can be integrated with document management systems such as SharePoint, Google Drive, and Box to automate tasks such as document processing, filing, and retrieval.

    UiPath's Integration Capabilities

    UiPath offers a wide range of integration capabilities to support the seamless integration of UiPath Robots with other systems and platforms. These capabilities include:

    • APIs and SDKs: UiPath offers APIs and SDKs that allow developers to integrate UiPath Robots with other systems and platforms, and to build custom connectors and integrations. Connectors and integrations: UiPath offers a variety of pre-built connectors and integrations for popular systems and platforms, including ERP, CRM, and HR systems, as well as document management systems and messaging platforms.

    • UiPath Marketplace: UiPath Marketplace is a community-driven marketplace that offers a variety of pre-built connectors, integrations, and automation components that can be easily downloaded and used in UiPath automation projects. By leveraging UiPath's integration capabilities, organizations can easily integrate UiPath Robots with other systems and platforms, and build end-to-end automation solutions that deliver real value to the business.

    Conclusion

    In conclusion, UiPath Robots offer a powerful and flexible solution for automating a wide range of tasks and processes. By leveraging the different types of UiPath Robots, organizations can automate tasks that range from simple and repetitive to complex and mission-critical. Moreover, UiPath Robots can be integrated with other systems and platforms, making them an essential component of end-to-end automation solutions.
